title: SceneCrafter: Controllable Multi-View Driving Scene Editing
title_zh: SceneCrafter：可控多视角驾驶场景编辑
abstract: Simulation is crucial for developing and evaluating autonomous vehicle (AV) systems. Recent literature builds on a new generation of generative models to synthesize highly realistic images for full-stack simulation. However, purely synthetically generated scenes are not grounded in reality and have difficulty in inspiring confidence in the relevance of its outcomes. Editing models, on the other hand, leverage source scenes from real driving logs, and enable the simulation of different traffic layouts, behaviors, and operating conditions such as weather and time of day. While image editing is an established topic in computer vision, it presents fresh sets of challenges in driving simulation: (1) the need for cross-camera 3D consistency, (2) learning “empty street” priors from driving data with foreground occlusions, and (3) obtaining paired image tuples of varied editing conditions while preserving consistent layout and geometry. To address these challenges, we propose SceneCrafter, a versatile editor for realistic 3D-consistent manipulation of driving scenes captured from multiple cameras. We build on recent advancements in multi-view diffusion models, using a fully controllable framework that scales seamlessly to multi-modality conditions like weather, time of day, agent boxes and high-definition maps. To generate paired data for supervising the editing model, we propose a novel framework on top of Prompt-to-Prompt [15] to generate geometrically consistent synthetic paired data with global edits. We also introduce an alpha-blending framework to synthesize data with local edits, leveraging a model trained on empty street priors through novel masked training and multi-view repaint paradigm. SceneCrafter demonstrates powerful editing capabilities and achieves state-of-the-art realism, controllability, 3D consistency, and scene editing quality compared to existing baselines.
abstract_zh: 仿真对于开发和评估自动驾驶系统至关重要。近期文献基于新一代生成模型，合成了高度逼真的图像用于全栈仿真。然而，纯合成场景缺乏现实基础，难以让人对其结果的相关性产生信心。另一方面，编辑模型利用真实驾驶日志中的源场景，能够模拟不同的交通布局、行为以及天气和时段等运行条件。虽然图像编辑在计算机视觉中是一个成熟课题，但在驾驶仿真中面临一系列新挑战：(1) 跨相机3D一致性的需求，(2) 从存在前景遮挡的驾驶数据中学习“空街道”先验，(3) 获取不同编辑条件下的成对图像元组，同时保持一致的布局和几何结构。为解决这些挑战，我们提出了SceneCrafter，一个用于多相机驾驶场景的通用编辑器，可实现逼真的3D一致性操控。我们基于多视角扩散模型的最新进展，构建了一个完全可控的框架，可无缝扩展到多模态条件，如天气、时段、智能体边界框和高清地图。为了生成用于监督编辑模型的成对数据，我们提出了一种基于Prompt-to-Prompt的新框架，生成具有全局编辑的几何一致的合成成对数据。我们还引入了一种alpha混合框架，用于合成局部编辑的数据，利用通过新颖的掩码训练和多视角重绘范式在空街道先验上训练的模型。SceneCrafter展示了强大的编辑能力，在逼真度、可控性、3D一致性和场景编辑质量方面均优于现有基线。
tldr_zh: 提出SceneCrafter，实现多相机驾驶场景的3D一致可控编辑。
cover_zh: 驾驶场景编辑新突破｜3D一致性+多模态控制，真实感拉满

>[!abstract] 一句话总结：提出SceneCrafter，基于多视图扩散模型，支持天气、时间、agent框、高精地图等多模态条件控制；利用Prompt-to-Prompt生成几何一致的全局编辑配对数据，并通过alpha-blending和空街先验模型实现局部编辑。


# 一、动机

> 现有纯合成场景缺乏真实感，而编辑模型面临跨相机3D一致性、学习空街先验、获取配对数据等挑战。


# 二、方法

> 提出SceneCrafter，基于多视图扩散模型，支持天气、时间、agent框、高精地图等多模态条件控制；利用Prompt-to-Prompt生成几何一致的全局编辑配对数据，并通过alpha-blending和空街先验模型实现局部编辑。


# 三、核心实验结果

> 与现有基线相比，在真实感、可控性、3D一致性和场景编辑质量上达到最先进水平，但未提供具体数值。


# 四、对我的启发

> 与课题不相关
